name: proof-aggregation
title: Recursive Proof Aggregation for Multi-Enterprise zkEVM L2
hypothesis: Recursive proof composition can aggregate proofs from N enterprise batches into a single proof verifiable on L1, reducing per-enterprise verification gas by N-fold while maintaining soundness guarantees, with aggregation overhead sub-linear in N.
null_hypothesis: Recursive aggregation overhead (prover time, circuit complexity, proof size) scales linearly or worse with N, making per-proof L1 verification more cost-effective than aggregation for N <= 16 enterprises.

naive_sequential.description: Verify proof_i inside proof_{i+1} circuit sequentially
naive_sequential.complexity: O(N) proving time, O(1) final proof size

binary_tree.description: Aggregate pairs recursively in a binary tree
binary_tree.complexity: O(log N) depth, O(N) total work, parallelizable

snarkpack_batch.description: Batch verify N Groth16/KZG proofs via inner-product argument
snarkpack_batch.complexity: O(N) verifier, O(log N) proof size overhead

nova_folding.description: Fold N instances incrementally via Nova/ProtoGalaxy
nova_folding.complexity: O(N) folding steps, O(1) final SNARK

success_criteria.gas_reduction: >= 4x reduction for N=8 enterprises vs individual verification
success_criteria.soundness: Aggregated proof valid iff all component proofs valid
